title: Amyema fitzgeraldii

The Loranthaceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world, including Australia and Southeast Asia.

It has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties.
